---
name: ddx
description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "what's blocking the queue", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
---

# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds;
Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery, aliases,
fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session rendering), a
persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library registry
(plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

Install locations: this skill is resolved from the project tier
(`ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx/`, which is `<project>/.ddx/plugins/ddx/` in-tree
or `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/ddx/` in convention
mode), the global machine layer (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`),
or the binary's baked-in default — see **Install topology** below. `ddx doctor`
reports which layer is active.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Install topology

DDx resolves the default `ddx` package in three layers: the project-local
tier at `ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx/` (in-tree or convention mode), then
the global install at `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, then the
baked-in package embedded in the binary. This is project > global > baked-in precedence.
`ddx doctor` reports the project and global layers separately so operators
can tell whether the project copy is real, missing, or lazily resolving to
the global layer.

For project installs, the agent-facing skill outputs live in
`<project>/.agents/skills/<name>/` and `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>/`.
For machine-wide installs (`ddx install <name> --global`), the plugin lands in
`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` and skill links are created under
`~/.agents/skills/<name>/` and `~/.claude/skills/<name>/`. `ddx install
--global` is reinstated under the zero-footprint epic; unmanaged home-directory
skill placements outside these managed paths are retired.

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
  `ddx bead status`.
- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  raw passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Worker** — a `ddx work` / `ddx try` process scoped to a specific project
  root. Do not call every `ddx work` process a "DDx worker" when multiple
  projects are visible. If the user says "DDx worker" without a qualifier,
  assume they mean the worker for the current/requested project. Say "worker
  for `<project>`" or "DDx-powered worker"; reserve "DDx repo worker" for work
  whose project root is the DDx repository.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
  Fizeau owns concrete route selection within those bounds.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Intent router

Before responding, read the matching file.

| User says / asks about | Read this file |
|---|---|
| "what should I work on next", "what's blocking the queue", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", broad queue orientation or planning | `reference/interactive.md` |
| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
| `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "start the worker", "start N workers and watch", "monitor the drain", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
| "assess bead readiness", "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |

If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. `ddx try` does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Power-first execution dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.

## Links out

- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
  executions, and skills features.
- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
- Open standard this skill conforms to:
  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).

It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. SQL migrations for auth/tenancy tables (FEAT-014, ADR-018).\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! Adds tables for:\\n4\\t//! - tenants: global account boundary\\n5\\t//! - users: global user identities\\n6\\t//! - user_identities: federated identity mapping\\n7\\t//! - tenant_users: M:N tenant membership\\n8\\t//! - tenant_databases: tenant-to-database mapping\\n9\\t//! - credential_revocations: revoked JWTs\\n10\\t//!\\n11\\t//! Note: `audit_retention_policies` is intentionally NOT included here — it\\n12\\t//! belongs to F1 audit-attribution (a separate bead / feature).\\n13\\t\\n14\\t/// Apply auth/tenancy migrations to a SQLite connection.\\n15\\t///\\n16\\t/// This is idempotent — running it multiple times on the same database\\n17\\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\\n18\\tpub fn apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: \u0026rusqlite::Connection) -\u003e Result\u003c(), String\u003e {\\n19\\t    // Create tenants table.\\n20\\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\\n21\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n22\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\\n23\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n24\\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\\n25\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n26\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n27\\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\\n28\\t        )\\\",\\n29\\t    )\\n30\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenants table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n31\\t\\n32\\t    // Create users table.\\n33\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n34\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\\n35\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n36\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n37\\t            email              TEXT,\\n38\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n39\\t            suspended_at_ms    INTEGER\\n40\\t        )\\\",\\n41\\t    )\\n42\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n43\\t\\n44\\t    // Create user_identities table.\\n45\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\\n46\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n47\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\\n48\\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n49\\t            external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\\n50\\t            user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n51\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n52\\t            PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\\n53\\t        )\\\",\\n54\\t    )\\n55\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"user_identities table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n56\\t\\n57\\t    // Create tenant_users table.\\n58\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required by ADR-018.\\n59\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n60\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_users (\\n61\\t            tenant_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n62\\t            user_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n63\\t            role         TEXT NOT NULL,\\n64\\t            added_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n65\\t            PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id)\\n66\\t        )\\\",\\n67\\t    )\\n68\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenant_users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n69\\t\\n70\\t    // Create tenant_databases table.\\n71\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n72\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases (\\n73\\t            tenant_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n74\\t            database_name  TEXT NOT NULL,\\n75\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n76\\t            PRIMARY KEY Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n36\\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//!             └─ route handler\\n38\\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//!                  └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\\n//!\\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\\n//! JwtIssuer {\\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e, issuer_id: String) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\\n\\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\\n    ///\\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\\n    pub fn verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e {\\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\\n decode::\u003cJwtClaims\u003e(token, \u0026DecodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret), \u0026validation)\\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\\n Three modes are supported:\\n//!\\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n//!\\n//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n//!\\n//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n//!\\n//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  └─ route handler\\n//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n//!                  └─ Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\\n\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::body::Body;\\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\\nuse axum::routing::get;\\nuse axum::Router;\\nuse http::Request;\\nuse serde::Serialize;\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{\\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\\n};\\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\\n    jwt_verify_layer,\\n};\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst ISSUER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"test-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"user-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"super-secret-key-for-tests\\\";\\nconst TEST_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_claims() -\u003e JwtClaims {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: Grants {\\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\\n            }],\\n        },\\n    }\\n}\\n\\nfn make_storage() -\u003e MemoryStorageAdapter {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Test User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    storage\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let storage: Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e =\\n        Box::new(make_storage());\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service call should succeed\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .expect(\\\"body read should succeed\\\");\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn get_req(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Request\u003cBody\u003e {\\n    Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(path)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer_req(path: \u0026str, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Request\u003cBody\u003e {\\n    Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(path)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\"))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn error_code(json: \u0026Value) -\u003e \u0026str {\\n    json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\")\\n}\\n\\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[test]\\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let claims = valid_claims();\\n    let token = issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\");\\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(\u0026token).expect(\\\"verify should succeed\\\");\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\\n}\\n\\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(error_code(\u0026json), \\\"unauthenticated\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", \\\"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\\\")\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(error_code(\u0026json), \\\"credential_malformed\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \\\"not.a.valid.jwt\\\");\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(error_code(\u0026json), \\\"credential_malformed\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\\n storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev ## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Federated nodes share bead queues through git, but nothing in the server-managed worker path ever runs fetch/merge/push — queues silently diverge. Observed live 2026-07-05/06:
- eitri's ddx checkout opened the session at main ahead 365 / behind 38 vs origin (weeks of worker closes never pushed; the retired /tmp factory-supervisor stopgap used to do per-cycle pull+push, server-managed workers do not).
- sindri's ddx checkout (queried via its server API /api/beads/status) reports total=2315 vs eitri's 2339 — it is missing 24 beads created on eitri including the P0 fixes ddx-57faa17c and ddx-a6e99ad3, and it holds 1 closed bead (closed=2206 vs 2205) never pushed to origin.
Latent failure: FEAT-026 partitions queue work by project owner, but ownership relies on both nodes seeing the same tracker. A worker started against a stale checkout re-executes already-closed beads or conflicts with landed work. FEAT-026's frame explicitly lists 'bead queues must be reconciled by hand' as the gap this bead closes.

# ROOT CAUSE
No component owns tracker git sync during autonomous drains. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go / the execute-bead loop land results to LOCAL main only; nothing fetches origin or pushes after close. The old stopgap supervisor script (per-cycle clean+pull/merge+push, see ddx-171dc478 stopgap) was retired when server-managed workers replaced it, dropping the sync side-effect.

# DIRECTIVE (operator, 2026-07-06)
Force a git sync BEFORE and AFTER claiming beads. The claim boundary — not just close/idle — is the sync point:
1. PRE-CLAIM: git fetch origin + merge origin/main --no-ff before candidate selection, so the worker picks from the freshest queue and never claims a bead another node already closed or claimed.
2. POST-CLAIM: commit + push the claim immediately, publishing it so other nodes' pre-claim syncs see the bead as taken. The cross-node double-execution window shrinks to the interval between one node's post-claim push and the other's pre-claim fetch.
3. POST-CLOSE/LAND: push the close and landed result (as before).
Idle-tick sync remains as a low-frequency backstop (default ~5min) for nodes that are not claiming.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement the three sync points above in the worker loop. Merge with --no-ff only (never squash/rebase; union-merge JSONL conflicts per existing tracker-merge conventions). Pre-claim sync failure (unreachable origin) degrades to claim-from-local with a warning event — availability over freshness, matching the worker-autonomy posture of ADR-022. Push failures after claim retry with backoff, then emit loop.operator_attention rather than blocking the drain. Respect LandCoordinator (skip merge while a land is in flight). Gate behind --tracker-sync (default ON for --watch), mirroring the --no-X discoverable-opt-out convention enforced by cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go.

# NON-SCOPE
- Hub-forwarded owner-targeted writes (FEAT-026 story 14 beads ddx-3f3e1cbe, ddx-20e7ebf0, ddx-0793ac75).
- Real-time distributed claim arbitration beyond the git claim protocol above (ownership partitioning remains the primary collision guard).
- Fixing sindri's currently-stale checkout (operational one-time reconcile; this bead prevents recurrence).

Related: FEAT-026, ADR-007-federation-topology, ddx-171dc478 (closed; safe-boundary precedent).

No deps.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncFetchesBeforeCandidateSelection proves: with a local bare fixture remote where origin/main closed bead X after the worker's last sync, the worker fetch+merges pre-claim and does NOT claim X (claims the next ready bead instead).
2. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostClaimPushPublishesClaim proves: immediately after claim, the claim commit is pushed; a second store cloned from origin sees the bead as in_progress before implementation begins.
3. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostCloseSyncPushesClose proves: after a landed close, origin's beads.jsonl contains the close.
4. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncDegradesWhenOriginUnreachable proves: with origin unreachable, the worker warns and proceeds claiming from local state (no stall), and TestExecuteLoop_TrackerSyncFailureEmitsOperatorAttention proves persistent post-claim push failure emits loop.operator_attention without blocking the drain.
5. Flag audit: --tracker-sync default-on for --watch with --no-tracker-sync opt-out passes cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go conventions.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TrackerSync|PreClaimSync|PostClaimPush' -count=1 green
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, area:federation, kind:feature, kind:reliability, phase:build, spec:FEAT-026</labels>     <metadata base-rev="ac62511420e32d0cfe0688fa9f168f636ccca2ac" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260706T143941-f864aa8a"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Every jam recovery on eitri 2026-07-06 was a manual git procedure (commit .ddx/executions dirt in the landing checkout; stash leaked attempt files matching a preserve ref; git worktree prune). These procedures live in operator memory, not code — so they are untested, unavailable to the supervisor, and cost hours of stall waiting for a human.

# PROPOSED FIX
New command 'ddx doctor --unjam' (project-scoped) that runs the remediation suite and reports each action taken:
1. Classify dirty/staged paths in the project root and landing checkout: (a) DDx-owned state (.ddx/executions/, .ddx/metrics/) -> commit as checkpoint; (b) paths whose content matches or derives from a refs/ddx/iterations/** preserve ref -> git stash push with a message naming the ref; (c) anything else -> report only, never touch.
2. git worktree prune + remove orphaned .ddx-exec-wt entries whose gitdir is gone (observed failure 2026-07-06T14:36: 'stat worktree .git: no such file' misclassified as resource_exhausted).
3. Clear stale claim leases (existing staleDiskEntryCanReleaseClaim path in cli/internal/server/workers.go).
4. Prune phantom deps (existing 'ddx bead doctor --fix' logic).
Idempotent; safe to run while a worker is live (respect LandCoordinator locks). Exit 0 with a machine-readable summary of actions; nonzero only on remediation errors.

# NON-SCOPE
Gate/supervisor wiring (sibling beads); new gate semantics.

Parent: ddx-9f21457d. No deps.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. TestDoctorUnjam_CheckpointsDdxOwnedDirt proves dirty .ddx/executions and .ddx/metrics paths are committed and the tree is then clean.

3. TestDoctorUnjam_PrunesStaleWorktrees proves a worktree registration with missing gitdir is pruned and a subsequent worktree add succeeds.

Federated nodes share bead queues through git, but nothing in the server-managed worker path ever runs fetch/merge/push — queues silently diverge. Observed live 2026-07-05/06:
- eitri's ddx checkout opened the session at main ahead 365 / behind 38 vs origin (weeks of worker closes never pushed; the retired /tmp factory-supervisor stopgap used to do per-cycle pull+push, server-managed workers do not).
- sindri's ddx checkout (queried via its server API /api/beads/status) reports total=2315 vs eitri's 2339 — it is missing 24 beads created on eitri including the P0 fixes ddx-57faa17c and ddx-a6e99ad3, and it holds 1 closed bead (closed=2206 vs 2205) never pushed to origin.
Latent failure: FEAT-026 partitions queue work by project owner, but ownership relies on both nodes seeing the same tracker. A worker started against a stale checkout re-executes already-closed beads or conflicts with landed work. FEAT-026's frame explicitly lists 'bead queues must be reconciled by hand' as the gap this bead closes.

# ROOT CAUSE
No component owns tracker git sync during autonomous drains. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go / the execute-bead loop land results to LOCAL main only; nothing fetches origin or pushes after close. The old stopgap supervisor script (per-cycle clean+pull/merge+push, see ddx-171dc478 stopgap) was retired when server-managed workers replaced it, dropping the sync side-effect.

# DIRECTIVE (operator, 2026-07-06)
Force a git sync BEFORE and AFTER claiming beads. The claim boundary — not just close/idle — is the sync point:
1. PRE-CLAIM: git fetch origin + merge origin/main --no-ff before candidate selection, so the worker picks from the freshest queue and never claims a bead another node already closed or claimed.
2. POST-CLAIM: commit + push the claim immediately, publishing it so other nodes' pre-claim syncs see the bead as taken. The cross-node double-execution window shrinks to the interval between one node's post-claim push and the other's pre-claim fetch.
3. POST-CLOSE/LAND: push the close and landed result (as before).
Idle-tick sync remains as a low-frequency backstop (default ~5min) for nodes that are not claiming.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement the three sync points above in the worker loop. Merge with --no-ff only (never squash/rebase; union-merge JSONL conflicts per existing tracker-merge conventions). Pre-claim sync failure (unreachable origin) degrades to claim-from-local with a warning event — availability over freshness, matching the worker-autonomy posture of ADR-022. Push failures after claim retry with backoff, then emit loop.operator_attention rather than blocking the drain. Respect LandCoordinator (skip merge while a land is in flight). Gate behind --tracker-sync (default ON for --watch), mirroring the --no-X discoverable-opt-out convention enforced by cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go.

# NON-SCOPE
- Hub-forwarded owner-targeted writes (FEAT-026 story 14 beads ddx-3f3e1cbe, ddx-20e7ebf0, ddx-0793ac75).
- Real-time distributed claim arbitration beyond the git claim protocol above (ownership partitioning remains the primary collision guard).
- Fixing sindri's currently-stale checkout (operational one-time reconcile; this bead prevents recurrence).

Related: FEAT-026, ADR-007-federation-topology, ddx-171dc478 (closed; safe-boundary precedent).

No deps.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncFetchesBeforeCandidateSelection proves: with a local bare fixture remote where origin/main closed bead X after the worker's last sync, the worker fetch+merges pre-claim and does NOT claim X (claims the next ready bead instead).
2. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostClaimPushPublishesClaim proves: immediately after claim, the claim commit is pushed; a second store cloned from origin sees the bead as in_progress before implementation begins.
3. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostCloseSyncPushesClose proves: after a landed close, origin's beads.jsonl contains the close.
4. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncDegradesWhenOriginUnreachable proves: with origin unreachable, the worker warns and proceeds claiming from local state (no stall), and TestExecuteLoop_TrackerSyncFailureEmitsOperatorAttention proves persistent post-claim push failure emits loop.operator_attention without blocking the drain.
5. Flag audit: --tracker-sync default-on for --watch with --no-tracker-sync opt-out passes cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go conventions.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TrackerSync|PreClaimSync|PostClaimPush' -count=1 green
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, area:federation, kind:feature, kind:reliability, phase:build, spec:FEAT-026</labels>     <metadata base-rev="ac62511420e32d0cfe0688fa9f168f636ccca2ac" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260706T143941-f864aa8a"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. - Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-f8582f42">     <title>doctor: add 'ddx doctor --unjam' — codified remediation suite for drain-halting state</title>     <description> # PROBLEM
Every jam recovery on eitri 2026-07-06 was a manual git procedure (commit .ddx/executions dirt in the landing checkout; stash leaked attempt files matching a preserve ref; git worktree prune). These procedures live in operator memory, not code — so they are untested, unavailable to the supervisor, and cost hours of stall waiting for a human.

# PROPOSED FIX
New command 'ddx doctor --unjam' (project-scoped) that runs the remediation suite and reports each action taken:
1. Classify dirty/staged paths in the project root and landing checkout: (a) DDx-owned state (.ddx/executions/, .ddx/metrics/) -> commit as checkpoint; (b) paths whose content matches or derives from a refs/ddx/iterations/** preserve ref -> git stash push with a message naming the ref; (c) anything else -> report only, never touch.

4. Prune phantom deps (existing 'ddx bead doctor --fix' logic).
Idempotent; safe to run while a worker is live (respect LandCoordinator locks). Exit 0 with a machine-readable summary of actions; nonzero only on remediation errors.

# NON-SCOPE
Gate/supervisor wiring (sibling beads); new gate semantics.
